title: Cymopterus trotteri
intro: Cymopterus trotteri, also known as Trotter's spring-parsley, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Cymopterus trotteri include sesquiterpenes, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tinctures and infusions, often used in traditional medicine to treat colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cymopterus trotteri is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States. Cymopterus trotteri is a perennial herb that produces small white or yellowish flowers in clusters. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Cymopterus, which is a group of plants commonly known as spring or winter onions. Cymopterus trotteri is also known by its common name, Trotter's spring onion.

properties: Cymopterus trotteri, also known as Trotter's spring-parsley, is a lesser-known plant with potential therapeutic properties.
